known_vcs.notes: Very active in Scotland, particularly tech/software
Edinburgh-based, strong Scottish focus. Merged with Praetura Ventures in 2025 to form PXN Ventures — see PXN Ventures entry.
Formed from the merger of Par Equity and Praetura Ventures in 2025. Co-led the £2.5m seed round for Quantcore alongside Blackfinch and Scottish Enterprise.
Fund manager for the British Business Bank's Investment Fund for Scotland. Highly active across sectors; appeared in Highway Data Systems, Nami Surgical, and Esk deals in May 2026.
European deeptech and climate VC. Led the £10m seed round for Edinburgh's Exergy3 in April 2026 — largest Scottish deal that run.

Glasgow-based deeptech accelerator and investor operating from Skypark. Invested in Quantcore, Vuabl, and Airspection as part of a £772k cohort round in early 2026. Aspires to be UK's most active deeptech investor.
Led the $3m pre-seed round for Edinburgh's Cnuic in April 2026. HQ uncertain — co-founder Martin Drdúl may be Central European.
Led the £1.9m growth round for Stirling-based Nami Surgical in January 2026. Very limited public profile.
Bavarian state-backed VC. Participated in the £10m seed round for Edinburgh's Exergy3 in April 2026.
Quantum-focused VC fund. Participated in the £2.5m seed round for Glasgow's Quantcore.
Participated in the £1.58m seed round for Edinburgh's BIOCAPTIVA in March 2026. Limited public profile.
Quasi-public, co-invests with VCs
UK-based climate/cleantech VC. Co-invested in Exergy3 £10m seed round (Edinburgh, April 2026).

Singapore-based private investment office. Impact/ESG lean — tagline 'Investing with purpose'. Co-invested in Exergy3 £10m seed round (Edinburgh, April 2026) alongside Axeleo Capital and Bayern Kapital.

Commercial subsidiary of the British Business Bank. Makes direct and fund investments to support UK smaller businesses. Co-invested in BIOCAPTIVA £1.58m seed round (Edinburgh, March 2026) alongside Archangels.
University of Glasgow's investment arm. Backs Glasgow spinouts at pre-seed and seed stage. Co-invested in Entourage AI $5m pre-seed (March 2026).

Female-founded London impact VC, FCA-regulated. Runs the Conduit EIS Impact Fund (est. 2021). Focus on sustainable industry, integrated health, and financial resilience. UK-only. Connected to the Conduit Club (4,000+ member network). Invested in Bioliberty £10.5m Series A (March 2026).
London-based fund (Cayman Islands structure). Existing investor in Bioliberty £10.5m Series A (March 2026). Very limited public profile — likely a family office or single-LP vehicle.

London-based angel syndicate focused on green/climate investments. Co-led Carbogenics $3m seed round (Edinburgh, January 2026). Backs early-stage cleantech, carbon, and energy transition companies across the UK.
US-based climate/cleantech VC. Co-led Carbogenics $3m seed round (Edinburgh, January 2026). Focus on carbon removal and nature-based solutions.
Led Entourage AI $5m pre-seed (Glasgow, March 2026). Likely UK-based but maintains a very low public profile — no website or other deals found. Healthtech/longevity focus inferred.
Biopharmaceutical-focused investor. Led EnteroBiotix £19m growth round (April 2026). Very limited public profile — may be a single-LP vehicle or family office.
Scotland's government-backed development bank, established 2020 with a £2bn mandate. Co-invests alongside private capital at Series A and above. Broad sector mandate — net zero, place, wellbeing.
Has Edinburgh office, growth-stage focus
Active across UK, some Scottish portfolio
EIS/SEIS focused, some Scottish deals
Scotland's oldest and largest angel syndicate. Website: archangelsonline.com. Leveraged £41m of investment in Scottish scaleups in 2025. Strong focus on life sciences, medtech, and software.
University of Edinburgh's venture fund, operating as part of Edinburgh Innovations (the university's commercialisation arm). Focuses on Edinburgh spinouts across deep tech, life sciences, and energy.
Has MEIF and other funds active in Scotland
Glasgow-based, West of Scotland focus
Impact/social enterprise focus
Life sciences specialist
University spinout specialist, active with Scottish universities
Edinburgh-based early-stage tech
Northern England focus, occasionally overlaps
Deep tech, has backed some Scottish spinouts
